title: What Is ScalpClock? A Trading Coach Built Into Your Charts
excerpt: ScalpClock pairs live scalp signals with a built-in Learn Hub, chart replay, and an Exit Assistant — so you build real skill while you trade, instead of guessing.

title: Why Chart Replay Helps Traders Improve Faster Than Live Trading Alone
excerpt: Live markets give you one shot per setup. Replay lets you rewind, re-run, and drill the same pattern until recognition becomes automatic.

title: How to Read Candlestick Patterns (Without Memorizing 50 Shapes)
excerpt: Most candlestick guides throw a wall of shapes at you. Here's the small set of patterns that actually show up often enough to matter, and how to read them.

title: 7 Scalping Mistakes New Traders Make (And How to Fix Them)
excerpt: Overtrading, chasing green candles, ignoring volume — the same handful of mistakes account for most blown-up scalping accounts. Here's how to spot them in your own trading.

title: Breakout Trading Explained: Spotting Real Breakouts vs. Fakeouts
excerpt: A breakout without volume is usually a trap. Here's how consolidation, volume, and follow-through combine to separate real breakouts from fakeouts.

title: Support and Resistance Made Simple
excerpt: Support and resistance aren't exact lines — they're zones where buyers and sellers have fought before. Here's how to draw them without overthinking it.

title: VWAP Trading Strategy Explained: Trading With the Institutional Average
excerpt: VWAP is the price institutions actually benchmark against. Here's how retail scalpers can use it to time entries instead of fighting the tape.

title: Moving Average Crossover Strategy: Golden Cross, Death Cross, and Scalping Timeframes
excerpt: Crossovers are simple, lagging, and still useful — if you know which timeframe you're trading and stop expecting them to call exact tops and bottoms.

title: How Traders Actually Build Pattern Recognition Skills
excerpt: Pattern recognition isn't memorization — it's repetition with feedback. Here's what the research on skill acquisition says, and how to apply it to charts.

title: ScalpClock vs. Traditional Trading Platforms: What's Actually Different
